name: Lima Bean, Leek and Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 2 cups dry lima beans, soaked for 2 to 4 hours
1 sprig fresh rosemary or 1/4 tsp dried rosemary
3 medium leeks, cut into quarters lengthwise
5 ears of corn (4 to 5 cups corn kernels)
1 sprig fresh rosemary or 1/4 tsp dried rosemary
3 tbsp white miso or salt to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain and rinse the lima beans. Place them in a 2-quart saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
2. Add the rosemary sprig or dried rosemary, leaving the lid slightly ajar. Lower the heat and gently simmer for 45 minutes to 1 hour until the beans are soft and creamy. Stir occasionally. Add more water as needed to keep the beans covered while cooking.
3. Slice leeks in half lengthwise, remove and discard the root end, and slice off the dark green tops, chopping them into 2-inch pieces. Chop the white and light green parts of the leek into 1/2-inch pieces and set aside.
4. Remove corn from the cob and reserve the kernels. Place the cobs, silk, and inner light green husks in a large soup pot with the water and rosemary. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for about 20 minutes.
5. Strain the leeks through a colander or into another pot or bowl, discarding the cobs, silk, husks, and tops. Return the strained leek pieces and a tablespoon of stock to the soup pot with salt. Stir over medium-low heat until the leeks have "sweated" and reduced in volume by half. If the leeks stick, add a little more stock.
6. Add the lima beans and corn kernels to the soup pot with the leeks. Add additional stock if needed. Simmer uncovered until the corn is tender, about 10 to 15 minutes.
7. If using miso instead of salt, mix it with 3 to 4 tablespoons hot stock in a small bowl or cup, then add this mixture back into the chowder and stir to blend.
8. Serve garnished with a tablespoon of salsa or chopped roasted red peppers, or a teaspoon of fresh herb or pesto. Serves 6.
Variation: Use 3 cups chopped onions instead of leeks, and make stock with 1 chopped onion, 1 chopped celery stalk, a few sprigs parsley, and the corn cobs. Per serving: 241 calories; 8g protein; 1g fat; 48g carbohydrates; 0 cholesterol; 40mg sodium; 11g fiber; vegan. Source: Vegetarian Times, Jan 94/MM by DEEANNE
